justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated with options and recommendation. Clock-start ambiguity identified in assumptions ('2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time') and in edge cases. Wrong-address exception from Finance noted as BR-6 and escalated as a separate ambiguity. Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict explicitly escalated as a contradiction. 8 business rules extracted with correct invariant classifications. 4 formal escalations provided. Confidence level of 38 is well below the 50 threshold.

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is the lead escalation with a well-reasoned hybrid recommendation
Auto-refund vs 3-business-day processing conflict identified as a separate formal escalation with practical resolution options
Wrong-address refund exception captured both as BR-6 and as a dedicated ambiguity escalation with attestation recommendation
Clock-start ambiguity addressed in assumptions and edge cases, though could have been a standalone escalation
8 business rules extracted — exceeds the 5 minimum — with thoughtful invariant classifications and testable assertions
Confidence at 38 with clear reasoning about why it's low, plus well-structured high/low confidence areas
Excellent edge cases covering boundary conditions, cascading failures, and temporal edge cases
Rejection reasons clearly explain why acceptance criteria cannot be defined yet
Clock-start ambiguity could have been elevated to a formal escalation rather than buried in assumptions — it materially affects the 2-hour SLA definition
BR-4 driver rating marked as invariant: false, which is defensible but the rubric expected invariant: true as a quality gate
